title: Anaphalioides mariae
intro: Anaphalioides mariae, also known as Mary's hair fern, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to New Zealand. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, as well as its ability to support digestive health and immune function. The bioactive constituents of Anaphalioides mariae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Anaphalioides mariae is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including colds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
